53.reason: The loop condition may cause segmentation fault if G[i].link is NULL for some i before reaching the sentinel node. Also, the sentinel node is only added for G[n_V].link, but the loop iterates over all i, potentially accessing uninitialized or NULL pointers.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [The loop condition G[i].link->adj!=-1 assumes every vertex has a link, but uninitialized vertices may have NULL link, causing segmentation fault. Also, the loop does not cover all vertices correctly.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  The loop condition may cause segmentation fault if G[i].link is NULL for some i before reaching the sentinel node. Also, the sentinel node is only added for G[n_V].link, but the loop iterates over all i, potentially accessing uninitialized or NULL pointers.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  循环条件依赖哨兵节点，可能访问越界且当顶点link为NULL时导致空指针错误
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  The loop condition G[i].link->adj!=-1 is incorrect. It assumes every vertex has a sentinel node with adj=-1, but more importantly, it fails to handle vertices that might become isolated or have NULL links correctly after deletion logic or if the sentinel wasn't added properly. Also, iterating up to n_V is safer. The sentinel approach in createGraph is non-standard and error-prone.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This loop condition assumes that every vertex's adjacency list ends with a sentinel node (-1), but not all lists may have it, leading to undefined behavior when accessing G[i].link->adj if G[i].link is NULL. Also, the loop doesn't handle the case where G[i].link is NULL before checking ->adj.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This loop condition assumes that every vertex's adjacency list ends with a sentinel node containing -1, but this sentinel is only added to G[n_V], which is out of bounds (vertices are 0 to n_V-1). Also, if G[i].link is NULL, accessing G[i].link->adj causes undefined behavior.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  If G[i].link is NULL (e.g., for the deleted vertex itself after setting it to NULL), dereferencing p->next will cause a segmentation fault.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [If G[i].link is NULL, this will crash. Also, the deletion logic doesn't correctly handle all cases: it only checks the head and one level deep in the list, and may miss nodes or cause memory leaks.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [If G[i].link is NULL or has only one node, this inner loop may not run, causing failure to remove the target vertex from adjacency lists properly.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  This check is inside the inner loop but should be checked before entering the loop; also, if the first node is the one to delete, the inner loop's condition p->next!=NULL will skip it.

94.reason: // 错误：应正确实现头插法，此处未更新头指针，导致链表顺序错误

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Should be e->next=head; not head->next. This incorrectly inserts the new node after head instead of before head.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.25 , reason:  This line incorrectly sets e->next to head->next instead of head, causing the new node to be inserted after head rather than before it. It should be e->next = head; head = e;
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  // 错误：应正确实现头插法，此处未更新头指针，导致链表顺序错误
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  应为 e->next = head; 当前实现未能正确插入头部
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Logic error: If inserting before head, e->next should be head, not head->next. This loses the rest of the list or creates a cycle/incorrect structure.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This is incorrect: when inserting before the head, e should point to the current head, not head->next. This breaks the sorted insertion and may skip the original head.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This should be e->next = head; head = e; to insert at the front. Current code skips the head node incorrectly.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  // 错误：同上，应改为 head = e; 并更新 e->next = head_old;
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  此处应改为 head = e;
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Logic error: Should update head to e. Here it keeps old head and appends e after it, but e->next was set to head->next. This effectively inserts e as the second node, not the first, violating sorted order if e < head.

125.reason: // 错误：当 n_V == MaxV 时数组越界，且哨兵设计有隐患

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This adds a sentinel node with adj=-1 to the last vertex's list, but it does not initialize link for other vertices. Also, the sentinel approach is flawed because it assumes all vertices have a link, but uninitialized ones have NULL.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  // 错误：当 n_V == MaxV 时数组越界，且哨兵设计有隐患

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  Error: Accessing G[n_V] is out of bounds if vertices are 0 to n_V-1. Also, adding a sentinel -1 node is problematic for traversal logic which expects valid vertex indices or NULL termination. It complicates deletion and traversal.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [This writes beyond the valid vertex range (0 to n_V-1). G[n_V] is out of bounds since vertices are 0..n_V-1. Also, adding a sentinel -1 is inconsistent with other parts of the code that don't expect it.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [G[n_V] is out of valid vertex range (0 to n_V-1). This writes beyond array bounds and creates an invalid sentinel.]

211.reason: [Should return Q[Front] after incrementing Front, not Q[Front-1]. This returns the wrong element.]
